questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given italicize word. Financial constraints forced the startup to halt its expansion plans temporarily.
questions.explanation: A) Constraint(noun) – limitation, restriction, obstacle, hindrance, bottleneck प्रतिबंध / बाधा Antonym: Freedom(noun) – The power or right to act, speak, or think as one wants. स्वतंत्रता Limitation(noun) – A restriction or boundary. सीमा Hindrance(noun) – An obstacle that prevents progress. रुकावट Obstacle(noun) – Something that blocks progress. बाधा

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word
questions.explanation: C) The incorrectly spelt word is C.Noctummbulism. The correct spelling isNoctambulism, which means "sleepwalking or walking during sleep". In Hindi, it translates to "नींद में चलने की प्रवृत्ति".

questions.question: Select the most appropriate phrase to replace the underlined word. She is feeling unwell today, so she will not be going to work.
questions.explanation: A)under the weather(idiom) – Feeling unwell or sick (बीमार या अस्वस्थ महसूस करना) B)face like thunder– Looking very angry (बहुत गुस्से में दिखना) C)head in the clouds– Daydreaming or not paying attention (ख्यालों में खोया हुआ या ध्यान न देना) D)chase rainbows– Pursue unrealistic dreams (अवास्तविक सपनों का पीछा करना)

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for highlighted group of words in the given sentence. He devoted his entire life to the study of the human mind.
questions.explanation: A)Psychology(noun) – The scientific study of the human mind and its behavior. मानसशास्त्र Physiology(noun) – The branch of biology dealing with the normal functions of living organisms and their parts. शरीर विज्ञान Physics(noun) – The branch of science concerned with the nature and properties of matter and energy. भौतिकी Philology (noun)– The study of language in written historical sources; a combination of literary studies, history, and linguistics.भाषाशास्त्र

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in theright order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. This means that decisions should not be taken under any kind of pressure to benefi tindividuals or only a small section of society. B. First, there should be transparency in making decisions; secrecy breeds corruption. C. There are certain values that civil servants need to imbibe in themselves for betteradministration. D. Then, all decisions must be taken only in public interest, to benefi t the community atlarge.
questions.explanation: D) CBDA C starts the sentence by introducing the subject "civil servants" and the main idea about the values they need to imbibe for better administration. B follows C as it introduces the first value ("transparency in making decisions") and provides a reason ("secrecy breeds corruption"), logically expanding on the idea introduced in C. D comes next as it introduces the second value ("decisions must be taken in public interest") using the transition word "Then," which indicates a sequential order of ideas. A concludes the sentence by explaining the implication of D ("This means..."), clarifying that decisions should not favor individuals or small sections of society, thus completing the argument. In Hindi:C sentence की शुरुआत करता है, क्योंकि यह subject "civil servants" और उनके लिए आवश्यक मूल्यों (values) को introduce करता है। B, C के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह पहला मूल्य ("decision-making में transparency") को बताता है और इसका कारण ("secrecy breeds corruption") देता है, जो C में बताई गई बात को expand करता है। D, B के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह transition word "Then" का use करके दूसरा मूल्य ("public interest में decisions") introduce करता है, जो ideas के logical sequence को दर्शाता है। A, D के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह "This means..." से शुरू होकर D में बताई गई बात को clarify करता है, यह बताते हुए कि decisions किसी individual या छोटे समूह के favor में नहीं होने चाहिए।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. I did not _______ that he would ______ the offer.
questions.explanation: D) 'Expect' का use होगा क्योंकि "expect" का अर्थ होता है किसी घटना या कार्य के होने की आशा या अनुमान लगाना। यहाँ sentence में mention है कि मैंने यह नहीं सोचा था कि वह इस प्रस्ताव को स्वीकार करेगा। 'Accept' का use होगा क्योंकि "accept" का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को स्वीकार करना। जबकि: 'Bait' का अर्थ है प्रलोभन देना। 'Bate' का अर्थ है कम करना या घटाना। 'Except' का अर्थ है छोड़कर, जो यहाँ context में सही नहीं है। 'Expect' will be used because it means to anticipate or think that something will happen. The sentence suggests that the speaker did not think he would accept the offer. 'Accept' will be used because it means to agree to or take something offered. 'Bait' means to lure or entice. 'Bate' means to reduce or lessen. 'Except' means excluding, which is not contextually correct here.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. My hands are full
questions.explanation: D)My hands are full(idiom) – To be very busy (बहुत व्यस्त होना)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. A majority of Indians prefer to use the Internet for ______________ booking and other financial services
questions.explanation: A)'Accessing'का use होगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ है किसी सेवा, डेटा, या संसाधन का use करना। sentence में mention है कि भारतीय लोग बुकिंग और अन्य वित्तीय सेवाओं के लिए इंटरनेट का use करते हैं। जबकि 'Improving' का अर्थ है सुधार करना, जो यहाँ संदर्भ में appropriate नहीं है। 'Attending' का अर्थ है उपस्थित होना, और 'Dispersing' का अर्थ है फैलाना या वितरित करना, जो इस वाक्य में उपयुक्त नहीं हैं। 'Accessing'will be used because it means to use or retrieve a service, data, or resource. The sentence specifies that Indians prefer using the Internet for bookings and other financial services, making 'accessing' the most appropriate option. Whereas, 'Improving' means enhancing or making better, which doesn’t fit here. 'Attending' means being present, and 'Dispersing' means spreading or distributing, both of which are irrelevant in this context.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. She confided in me on her anxious, whispering her secrets between sips of coffee
questions.explanation: C) ‘about her anxiety" है क्योंकि यहाँ "about" का use किसी विषय के संदर्भ में करने के लिए उपयुक्त है। "anxious" एक adjective है और इसे noun form "anxiety" में बदलने की आवश्यकता है, ताकि वाक्य अर्थपूर्ण और व्याकरणिक रूप से सही हो। The correct answer is "about her anxiety" because "about" is the appropriate preposition to indicate the subject, and "anxiety" is the correct noun form to describe the feeling.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given italicize word. Handling chemicals without gloves can be extremely hazardous to your health.
questions.explanation: A) Hazardous (adjective)– dangerous, risky, unsafe, toxic, harmfulखतरनाकSynonym:Toxic(adjective) – Poisonous or harmful(विषाक्त)Harmless(adjective) – Not dangerous(हानिरहित)Gentle(adjective) – Mild or kind(नम्र)Useful(adjective) – Helpful or beneficial(उपयोगी)

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word
questions.explanation: A) The incorrectly spelt word is ‘Credeble’. The correct spelling is ‘Credible’, which means “able to be believed; convincing” in English, and in Hindi, it means "विश्वसनीय".

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given phrase. A person who does not believe in the institution of marriage
questions.explanation: B)Misogamist(noun) – A person who does not believe in the institution of marriage. विवाह संस्था में विश्वास न रखने वाला व्यक्ति Misogynist(noun) – A person who dislikes or is strongly prejudiced against women. महिलाओं से घृणा करने वाला व्यक्ति Misogamist(noun) – A person who does not believe in the institution of marriage. विवाह संस्था में विश्वास न रखने वाला व्यक्ति Masochist(noun) – A person who derives pleasure, especially sexual gratification, from their own pain or humiliation. दुख या अपमान से आनंद पाने वाला व्यक्ति

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given italicize word. The doctor’s altruistic work in rural villages earned him the community's eternal gratitude.
questions.explanation: D) Altruistic(adjective) – selfless, charitable, generous, humanitarian, philanthropic परोपकारी Synonym: Generous(adjective) – Willing to give or share, benevolent. उदार Selfish(adjective) – Concerned primarily with one's own interests. स्वार्थी Proud(adjective) – Feeling pleasure or satisfaction in one’s achievements. गर्वित Indifferent(adjective) – Not caring, neutral, unconcerned. उदासीन

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Dig up dirt
options: To uncover negative information about someone or something.
questions.explanation: C)Dig up dirt– To uncover negative information about someone or something किसी व्यक्ति या चीज़ के बारे में नकारात्मक जानकारी निकालना।

questions.question: Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error. My aunt came from Switzerland by the flight which was landing at 5:00 a.m. yesterday
questions.explanation: D) 'was landing' के बदले 'landed' का use होगा क्योंकि यहाँ Past Simple Tense का use उचित है, क्योंकि यह एक completed action को दर्शाता है जो निश्चित समय (5:00 a.m. yesterday) में हुई। 'Was landing' (Past Continuous) का प्रयोग अनावश्यक है क्योंकि यहाँ कोई अन्य parallel action नहीं हो रही है। 'landed' will be used instead of 'was landing' because Past Simple Tense is appropriate here, as it indicates a completed action that happened at a definite time (5:00 a.m. yesterday). 'Was landing' (Past Continuous) is unnecessary because there is no other parallel action happening here.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given italicize word. The magician made the rabbit vanish from the hat in front of the astonished crowd.
questions.explanation: B)Vanish(verb) – Disappear, fade, dissolve, evaporate, go away गायब होनाAntonym:Appear(verb) – To come into sight, become visible. प्रकट होना Disappear(verb) – To go out of sight, vanish. गायब होना Evaporate(verb) – To change from a liquid to a gas, vanish. वाष्पित होना Fade(verb) – To gradually disappear or lose color. हल्का होना

questions.question: Select the option that best expresses the meaning of the given idiom. When pigs fly
questions.explanation: D)When pigs fly (idiom)- Just impossiblemeans something that will never happen or is impossible., इसका अर्थ है "ऐसा जो कभी नहीं हो सकता।"

questions.question: In the following sentence the underlined part contains as error. Alternatives to the underlined part are given as options. Select the correct alternative. The water near the coast was shallower and clean
questions.explanation: D) "was shallower and clean" में error है। यहाँ "shallower" (तुलनात्मक डिग्री) का प्रयोग गलत है क्योंकि "clean" (सकारात्मक डिग्री) के साथ parallel structure बनाने के लिए "shallow" (सकारात्मक डिग्री) का use होना चाहिए। "was shallower and clean" contains an error. Here, "shallower" (comparative degree) is incorrect because to maintain parallel structure with "clean" (positive degree), "shallow" (positive degree) should be used. "Shallower" (comparative) would be correct only if comparing two things, but here "clean" is in the positive degree, so "shallow" (positive) must be used for parallelism. Parallel Structure Rule: When two adjectives are connected by "and", they should be in the same degree.

questions.question: Select the grammatically correct sentence from the given options
options: She was not allowed to enter the hotel as her clothes was dirtier.
She was not allowed to enter the hotel as her clothes were dirtier.
She was not allowed to enter the hotel as her clothes were dirty.
She was not allow to enter the hotel as her clothes were dirty.
questions.explanation: C) 'clothes' एक plural noun है, इसलिए इसके साथ 'was' का उपयोग करना गलत है। इसके स्थान पर 'were' का उपयोग होना चाहिए। Clothes' is a plural noun, so using 'was' is incorrect. Instead, 'were' should be used.

questions.question: Given below are four sentences in jumbled order. Pick the option that gives their correct order. A. Her face had the most extraordinary expression of horror and amusement. B. My mother looked up in time to see me fall. C. But I missed and went sprawling across the kitchen floor. D. My hand reached out to grab the handle of the door that led into the kitchen.
questions.explanation: C) DCBA D starts the sentence with the subject “My hand”, which introduces the action being performed (reaching out to grab the handle). This sets the scene for the sequence of events. C follows D because it logically continues the action—the hand missed the handle, leading to the consequence (falling). The word “But” indicates a contrast between the intended action (grabbing the handle) and the actual outcome (falling). B comes next as it shifts focus to “My mother”, who witnessed the fall. This introduces a new subject and reaction to the previous action. A concludes the sequence by describing “Her face” (mother’s reaction), which is a natural progression after stating that she saw the fall. The phrase “the most extraordinary expression” provides a vivid ending to the sequence. D sentence की शुरुआत करता है, क्योंकि यह subject “My hand” को introduce करता है, जो action (दरवाज़े का हैंडल पकड़ने की कोशिश) को दर्शाता है। C, D के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह “But” से contrast दिखाता है—हाथ ने हैंडल नहीं पकड़ा, जिसके कारण गिरना हुआ। यह एक logical consequence है। B, C के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह एक नए subject (“My mother”) को introduce करता है, जो गिरते हुए देखती है। A sentence को समाप्त करता है, क्योंकि यह “Her face” (माँ के चेहरे के भाव) को describe करता है, जो B में बताए गए action (देखना) का ही result है।

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The young seagull was alone on his ____1_____. His two brothers and his sister had already flown away the day before. He had been afraid to fly with them. Somehow when he had taken a little run forward to the brink of the ledge and attempted to flap his wings he became afraid. The great expanse of sea stretched down ______2_____, and it was such a long way down — miles down. He felt certain that his wings would never support him; so he bent his head and ran away back to the little hole under the ledge where he slept at night. Even when each of his brothers and his little sister, whose wings were far shorter than his own, ran to the brink, flapped their wings, and flew away, he failed to muster up courage to take that plunge which appeared to him so desperate. His father and mother had come around calling to him shrilly, upbraiding him, threatening to let him starve on his ledge unless he flew away. But for the life of him he could not move. That was twenty-four hours ago. ____3_____then nobody had come near him. The day before, all day long, he had watched his parents flying about with his brothers and sister, perfecting them in the art of flight, teaching them how to skim the waves and how to dive for fish. He had, in fact, seen his older brother catch his first herring and devour it, standing on a rock, ____4_____his parents circled around raising a proud cackle. And all the morning the whole family ___________5__________walked about on the big plateau midway down the opposite cliff taunting him with his cowardice. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: D) 'Ledge' का use होगा क्योंकि "ledge" का अर्थ होता है चट्टान का किनारा। Sentence में mention किया गया है कि युवा सीगल अपने चट्टान के किनारे पर अकेला था। इस context में, 'ledge' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Abridge' का अर्थ है संक्षेप करना, 'Nudge' का अर्थ है हल्का धक्का देना, और 'Upsurge' का अर्थ है वृद्धि, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Ledge' will be used because it means a narrow horizontal surface projecting from a wall, cliff, or other surface. The sentence mentions that the young seagull was alone on his ledge, which is a suitable context for 'ledge.' Whereas, 'Abridge' means to shorten, 'Nudge' means to give a gentle push, and 'Upsurge' means a rapid increase, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The young seagull was alone on his ____1_____. His two brothers and his sister had already flown away the day before. He had been afraid to fly with them. Somehow when he had taken a little run forward to the brink of the ledge and attempted to flap his wings he became afraid. The great expanse of sea stretched down ______2_____, and it was such a long way down — miles down. He felt certain that his wings would never support him; so he bent his head and ran away back to the little hole under the ledge where he slept at night. Even when each of his brothers and his little sister, whose wings were far shorter than his own, ran to the brink, flapped their wings, and flew away, he failed to muster up courage to take that plunge which appeared to him so desperate. His father and mother had come around calling to him shrilly, upbraiding him, threatening to let him starve on his ledge unless he flew away. But for the life of him he could not move. That was twenty-four hours ago. ____3_____then nobody had come near him. The day before, all day long, he had watched his parents flying about with his brothers and sister, perfecting them in the art of flight, teaching them how to skim the waves and how to dive for fish. He had, in fact, seen his older brother catch his first herring and devour it, standing on a rock, ____4_____his parents circled around raising a proud cackle. And all the morning the whole family ___________5__________walked about on the big plateau midway down the opposite cliff taunting him with his cowardice. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: A) Beneath' का use होगा क्योंकि "beneath" का अर्थ होता है किसी वस्तु के सीधे नीचे। Sentence में कहा गया है कि समुद्र का बड़ा विस्तार उसके नीचे फैला हुआ था। 'Beneath' इस context में सही है क्योंकि यह दर्शाता है कि समुद्र सीगल के ठीक नीचे फैला हुआ था। जबकि 'Underneath' का अर्थ भी नीचे होता है लेकिन इसका उपयोग अक्सर किसी वस्तु के सीधे संपर्क में होने के लिए होता है, जो यहां उचित नहीं है। 'Under' का अर्थ भी नीचे होता है, लेकिन यह साधारण और सीधे तौर पर नीचे के लिए होता है 'Above' का अर्थ होता है ऊपर, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Beneath' will be used because it means directly under something, and it is appropriate in literary contexts. The sentence states that the great expanse of sea stretched down beneath him. 'Beneath' is grammatically correct here as it suggests the sea is directly below the seagull. Whereas, 'Underneath' also means below but is usually used to indicate something being directly under another object and often implies direct contact, which is not the case here. 'Under' is a simpler form of below and not as literary, and 'Above' means over, which does not fit the context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The young seagull was alone on his ____1_____. His two brothers and his sister had already flown away the day before. He had been afraid to fly with them. Somehow when he had taken a little run forward to the brink of the ledge and attempted to flap his wings he became afraid. The great expanse of sea stretched down ______2_____, and it was such a long way down — miles down. He felt certain that his wings would never support him; so he bent his head and ran away back to the little hole under the ledge where he slept at night. Even when each of his brothers and his little sister, whose wings were far shorter than his own, ran to the brink, flapped their wings, and flew away, he failed to muster up courage to take that plunge which appeared to him so desperate. His father and mother had come around calling to him shrilly, upbraiding him, threatening to let him starve on his ledge unless he flew away. But for the life of him he could not move. That was twenty-four hours ago. ____3_____then nobody had come near him. The day before, all day long, he had watched his parents flying about with his brothers and sister, perfecting them in the art of flight, teaching them how to skim the waves and how to dive for fish. He had, in fact, seen his older brother catch his first herring and devour it, standing on a rock, ____4_____his parents circled around raising a proud cackle. And all the morning the whole family ___________5__________walked about on the big plateau midway down the opposite cliff taunting him with his cowardice. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: D) 'Since' का उपयोग होगा क्योंकि "since" का अर्थ है किसी निश्चित समय से अब तक। Sentence में "That was twenty-four hours ago. Since then nobody had come near him." के रूप में है। 'Since' इस context में सही है क्योंकि यह दर्शाता है कि उस घटना के बाद से अब तक कोई उसके पास नहीं आया। जबकि 'While' का अर्थ है "के दौरान", जो यहां समय का एक निश्चित बिंदु नहीं दर्शाता। 'From' समय की शुरुआत का संकेत देता है लेकिन अवधि की निरंतरता को नहीं दर्शाता। 'When' का अर्थ है "जब," जो यहां उपयुक्त नहीं है क्योंकि यह समय की निरंतरता को नहीं दर्शाता। 'Since' will be used because "since" indicates a point in time from the past until now. The sentence is structured as "That was twenty-four hours ago. Since then nobody had come near him." 'Since' is grammatically correct here as it shows continuity from that point in time until now. Whereas, 'While' means "during," which does not indicate a specific point in time. 'From' indicates the starting point but does not imply continuity over time. 'When' means "at the time that," which does not fit here because it does not convey the ongoing nature of the action.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The young seagull was alone on his ____1_____. His two brothers and his sister had already flown away the day before. He had been afraid to fly with them. Somehow when he had taken a little run forward to the brink of the ledge and attempted to flap his wings he became afraid. The great expanse of sea stretched down ______2_____, and it was such a long way down — miles down. He felt certain that his wings would never support him; so he bent his head and ran away back to the little hole under the ledge where he slept at night. Even when each of his brothers and his little sister, whose wings were far shorter than his own, ran to the brink, flapped their wings, and flew away, he failed to muster up courage to take that plunge which appeared to him so desperate. His father and mother had come around calling to him shrilly, upbraiding him, threatening to let him starve on his ledge unless he flew away. But for the life of him he could not move. That was twenty-four hours ago. ____3_____then nobody had come near him. The day before, all day long, he had watched his parents flying about with his brothers and sister, perfecting them in the art of flight, teaching them how to skim the waves and how to dive for fish. He had, in fact, seen his older brother catch his first herring and devour it, standing on a rock, ____4_____his parents circled around raising a proud cackle. And all the morning the whole family ___________5__________walked about on the big plateau midway down the opposite cliff taunting him with his cowardice. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4
questions.explanation: A) While' का use होगा क्योंकि "while" का अर्थ होता है "के दौरान" या "जबकि," जो यहां उचित है। Sentence में mention किया गया है कि जब उसके बड़े भाई ने अपनी पहली हेरिंग पकड़ी और खा ली, तब उसके माता-पिता उसके चारों ओर गर्व से घूम रहे थे। 'Which' का अर्थ है "जो," 'When' का अर्थ है "जब," और 'Where' का अर्थ है "जहाँ," जो इस context में सही नहीं हैं। 'While' should be used because it means "during the time that" or "at the same time," which is appropriate here. The sentence mentions that the older brother caught and devoured his first herring, "while" his parents circled around raising a proud cackle. 'Which' means "which," 'When' means "when," and 'Where' means "where," which do not fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The young seagull was alone on his ____1_____. His two brothers and his sister had already flown away the day before. He had been afraid to fly with them. Somehow when he had taken a little run forward to the brink of the ledge and attempted to flap his wings he became afraid. The great expanse of sea stretched down ______2_____, and it was such a long way down — miles down. He felt certain that his wings would never support him; so he bent his head and ran away back to the little hole under the ledge where he slept at night. Even when each of his brothers and his little sister, whose wings were far shorter than his own, ran to the brink, flapped their wings, and flew away, he failed to muster up courage to take that plunge which appeared to him so desperate. His father and mother had come around calling to him shrilly, upbraiding him, threatening to let him starve on his ledge unless he flew away. But for the life of him he could not move. That was twenty-four hours ago. ____3_____then nobody had come near him. The day before, all day long, he had watched his parents flying about with his brothers and sister, perfecting them in the art of flight, teaching them how to skim the waves and how to dive for fish. He had, in fact, seen his older brother catch his first herring and devour it, standing on a rock, ____4_____his parents circled around raising a proud cackle. And all the morning the whole family ___________5__________walked about on the big plateau midway down the opposite cliff taunting him with his cowardice. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5
questions.explanation: A) Had ‘Had’ का use होगा क्योंकि ‘had walked’ भूतकाल में पहले पूरी हो चुकी क्रिया (सुबह-भर चला जाना) को दिखाता है; जबकि ‘has’ वर्तमान तक चलने वाले कार्य को, ‘was’ और ‘were’ continuous रूप माँगते हैं (was/were walking), इसलिए ये संदर्भ और व्याकरण दोनों में गलत हैं।• ‘Had’ will be used because ‘had walked’ forms the past perfect, marking an action finished before another past event. ‘Has’ (present perfect) drags the action toward the present, and ‘was/were’ need the –ing form (was/were walking); thus they do not fit the context or grammar here.
